nameMeanings.name: Pine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English pin(e) (Old English pīn, Old French pin), a topographic name for someone who lived by a conspicuous pine tree or in a pine forest; in some cases it may originally have been a nickname for a tall man, one thought to resemble a pine tree.
English: nickname from Middle English pine ‘pain, injury, torture, punishment, infirmity’, referring to someone who suffered from an inflicted pain or punishment.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Le Pin in Calvados or some other French place called from a prominent pine.

variants.name: Pyne
meanings: English: variant of   Pine  . This name has been established in Ireland since the 16th century.

variants.name: Peine
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Hermann, Markus.
German: habitational name from Peine near Hanover.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Pine was born about 1135, in Hooton Pagnell, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Sir John de Luttrell II, Lord of Hooton Pagnell in Hooton Pagnell, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in England, United Kingdom.

ko.Hooton Pagnell, South Yorkshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 소우스 요르크실르 후턴 파그넬
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Pine(은)는 대략 1135년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 후턴 파그넬에서 태어났다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 후턴 파그넬에서 Sir John de Luttrell II, Lord of Hooton Pagnell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John de Luttrell II, Lord of Hooton Pagnell в местности хутон пагнелл, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь.
